title: Dacrydium cornwallianum
taxonomy: Dacrydium cornwallianum is a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and is also known as the Cornwallia pine. The tree is a part of the genus Dacrydium, which is a group of conifers that are widely distributed in tropical and subtropical regions. Dacrydium cornwallianum is a medium-sized tree that can grow up to 10-15 meters tall. The tree's classification is further supported by its shared characteristics with other species in the Podocarpaceae family.

intro: Dacrydium cornwallianum is a species of coniferous tree, classified under the family Podocarpaceae, native to tropical Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and other infections. The bioactive constituents of Dacrydium cornwallianum include terpenoids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
